mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates compress equity discount rates and increase market liquidity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals severe labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real GDP and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in aggregate demand, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative return differential compared to baseline over 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns during financial stress periods.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in 70% of observations when industrial production declines during stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 3.5 percent annually forces markets to price in aggressive future rate hikes, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 12-month window or 5-day forward returns are positive in 65% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payrolls contraction and rising unemployment confirm structural labor market weakness, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.005 or fails to predict negative returns in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate robust consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in 60% of observations when retail sales increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals recovering manufacturing demand and improved corporate margins, producing positive equity returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative 5-day forward returns in 65% of triggered instances over a 36-month rolling evaluation.
